summary: Parallel review: FAIL (1 critical, 6 warning, 15 suggestion). Compound gate: PASS

findingsList.text: 🟡 .team/features/git-worktree-isolation/tasks/task-3/ — Missing `artifacts/test-output.txt`; capture gate stdout for verification-only tasks (backlog).

🟡 bin/lib/run.mjs:53 — `runGateInline(cmd, featureDir, taskId, cwd = process.cwd())` retains an implicit `process.cwd()` default fallback, contrary to the spec wording. Latent footgun; drop the default to make `cwd` required.
🟡 bin/lib/run.mjs:53 — Default `cwd = process.cwd()` still allows implicit fallback; drop the default or throw on undefined to enforce the spec
🟡 bin/lib/run.mjs:282 — `dispatchToAgent` does not validate `cwd`; passing undefined silently inherits process.cwd() via spawnSync. Add a required-arg guard
🟡 test/worktree.test.mjs:181 — Add a negative test (cwd omitted → throws/detectable) to lock in the "no implicit fallback" contract
🟡 bin/lib/run.mjs:53 — Drop the `cwd = process.cwd()` default (or throw on falsy) so the worktree boundary is enforced, not just assumed

No 🔴 / 🟡 from the simplicity lens. All four veto categories (dead code, premature abstraction, unnecessary indirection, gold-plating) pass — the PR adds no code, so by definition introduces no new complexity. Eval written to `.team/features/git-worktree-isolation/tasks/task-3/eval.md`.
